title: Octets, Byte Streams, Fragmentation, Buffering, and Partial I/O · Runtime Recall
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs octets, byte streams, fragmentation, buffering, and partial i/o from bounded byte and state evidence, challenges one misconception, and revises the fragment-safe byte-stream reader and writer contract. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for fragment-safe byte-stream reader and writer contract after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Keep protocol data as octets until grammar permits text interpretation and distinguish ASCII tokens, opaque content, and invalid control bytes.
Interpret Read results using both byte count and error, including data with io.EOF, zero-byte reads, and temporary fragmentation.
Complete bounded output across short writes while retaining progress, error, and ownership evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Octet Text Boundary
steps.instruction:
Order the Octet Text Boundary evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Octet Text Boundary.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Octet Text Boundary assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Octet Text Boundary, keep Octet Text Boundary connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.text: A review protocol team handles case 9797 in a privacy-sensitive proxy translating between two parser implementations. Build deterministic Go evidence to preserve partial bytes while distinguishing fragmentation from incomplete input; preserve this operating constraint: no malformed framing may be normalized and forwarded to another recipient; then split every structural delimiter across a different read and retain the same result. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Keep protocol data as octets until grammar permits text interpretation and distinguish ASCII tokens, opaque content, and invalid control bytes.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Octet Text Boundary has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only.
